visual_checks: At 320 CSS pixels width (equivalent to 400% zoom on a 1280px wide desktop), check if content reflows into a single column without horizontal scrolling
Look for content that is cut off or hidden at narrow widths
Check for horizontal scrollbars appearing when zoomed to 400% at 1280px viewport
Look for overlapping text or UI elements at narrow widths
Verify navigation menus adapt (hamburger menu or vertical stacking) rather than getting cut off
Check data tables, images, and embedded content for horizontal overflow
pass_conditions: Content can be presented at 320 CSS pixels wide without requiring horizontal scrolling for vertically-scrolling content
Content can be presented at 256 CSS pixels high without requiring vertical scrolling for horizontally-scrolling content
All content and functionality is available without two-dimensional scrolling (except content that requires 2D layout: data tables, images, maps, diagrams, video, toolbars)
Text reflows into a single column at narrow widths
Navigation adapts to narrow widths (e.g., collapsing to a hamburger menu)
fail_conditions: Horizontal scrollbar appears at 320px width for content that should reflow
Text or content is clipped/hidden at 320px width with no way to access it
Content overlaps making it unreadable at 320px width
Fixed-width layouts that do not adapt to narrow viewports
Two-column layouts that do not collapse to single column, requiring horizontal scrolling to read
Sticky headers or footers that take up too much vertical space at narrow widths, leaving little room for content
na_conditions: Content inherently requires two-dimensional scrolling for understanding: data tables, maps, diagrams, video players, toolbars, drawing canvases
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Page was only tested at one viewport width and 320px was not evaluated
Cannot verify reflow behavior from a single screenshot without resizing
auditor_anti_patterns: FALSE POSITIVE (CRITICAL — invented CSS value): inventing CSS class names, custom-property values, style attributes, or pixel widths. Every value you cite (e.g. `--grid-column-count:2`, `width:373px`, `is-nowrap`) MUST appear verbatim in the dom.html in the user prompt. If dom.html shows `style="--grid-column-count:1"`, do NOT write `--grid-column-count:2` in your finding. Quote the actual value or do not report the finding. A finding citing the wrong CSS value misleads developers and erodes trust in the entire ACR.

FALSE POSITIVE: claiming reflow failure based purely on the desktop screenshot. The mobile/narrow rendering is captured separately (viewport_320px.png if present). If you do not have evidence at 320px width, mark Not Evaluated and explain — do not extrapolate from desktop.

FALSE POSITIVE: flagging content that is exempt because it inherently requires 2D layout (data tables, maps, complex diagrams, video players). Confirm the content type before reporting reflow failure.
common_mistakes: Using fixed-width containers (width: 960px) instead of max-width or percentage-based widths
Absolutely positioned elements that overflow their containers at narrow widths
Images without max-width: 100% that extend beyond the viewport
CSS that hides content at narrow widths using display:none in media queries (content is lost, not reflowed)
Horizontal navigation bars that do not collapse into a mobile menu
Form layouts that stay side-by-side instead of stacking vertically

examples.pass: At 400% zoom (320 CSS px width), the page content reflows into a single readable column, the navigation collapses to a hamburger menu, images scale down, and no horizontal scrollbar appears
examples.fail: At 400% zoom, the page maintains its 960px fixed layout, requiring extensive horizontal scrolling to read each line of text — users must scroll right and left for every line
examples.partial: Most content reflows correctly at 320px but a sidebar widget maintains its fixed width and creates a horizontal scrollbar, and one form has side-by-side fields that overlap

plain_meaning: Content must reflow so users do not have to scroll in two directions at once. At a viewport of 320 CSS pixels wide, there should be no horizontal scrolling for vertically-scrolling content (and vice versa for horizontally-scrolling content at 256 CSS pixels tall). Exceptions exist for content that genuinely requires a two-dimensional layout such as maps, complex data tables, diagrams, and some code blocks.
scope_applies_to: Page layouts at 320 CSS px width (equivalent to 400% zoom on a 1280px viewport)
Navigation, content, forms, and interactive regions
Text columns and grids that should stack at narrow widths
Sticky headers and fixed regions that may overflow at narrow widths
Modal dialogs and popovers shown at narrow viewports
scope_does_not_apply_to: Visually-hidden / sr-only text (has no rendered box that could cause horizontal scroll)
Elements removed from the accessibility tree (aria-hidden, display:none)
Content that requires two-dimensional layout to be usable: maps, complex data tables, diagrams, video players with overlay controls, sheet-music, large images meant to be panned
Code samples and preformatted blocks where line wrapping would destroy meaning
borderline_calibration: Minor horizontal overflow of a few pixels caused by padding or scrollbars is not a clear failure; verify it affects perceivable content.
Two-dimensional-essential content is exempt; do not flag a map widget or data table for requiring horizontal scroll.
Sticky headers that cause content to be obscured but not cause 2-D scroll are not a 1.4.10 failure (they may touch 1.4.12 or 2.4.11).
Do NOT flag sr-only / visually-hidden text as reflow failures.
When in doubt, evaluate at exactly 320 px width and look for an actual horizontal scrollbar on main content.
